name: Systemic Weather
description: Systemic Weather is a systemic mechanic where entities update according to rules and create emergent state over time.
design_notes: Systemic Weather should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For simulation mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use systemic weather as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect systemic weather to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels the world continues to operate through understandable systems.

parameters.name: tick_rate
parameters.description: Frequency at which the simulation updates.

parameters.name: entity_count
parameters.description: Number of entities the mechanic is expected to support.

parameters.name: rule_weight
parameters.description: Strength of one rule compared with other simulation rules.

edge_cases: Systemic Weather is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Systemic Weather overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Systemic Weather runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Systemic Weather state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Systemic Weather plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Systemic Weather produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Systemic Weather strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Systemic Weather becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Systemic Weather with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Systemic Weather as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Systemic Weather with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Systemic Weather as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.

example_games.title: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
example_games.note: Uses weather to affect traversal and combat choices.
